title: Addition and subtraction word problems 1
description: Practice adding and subtracting to solve word problems. Numbers used are 20 or less.

assessment_items.question_content:
$8$ witches are flying on their broomsticks. $3$ land on the ground.


**Which choice can be used to find how many witches are still flying?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blue{3}$ witches land so we use subtraction to take away $\blue{3}$.


$\red{8} - \blue{3}$

assessment_items.question_content:
A fisher caught $16$ fish today. On her way back to the shore, a whale jumped over her boat and ate $6$ of the fish.


**Which choice can be used to find how many fish she has left on her boat?**



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The whale ate $\blue6$ fish so we use subtraction to take away $\blue6$.

$\red{16} - \blue6$

assessment_items.question_content:
$12$ fireflies were having a party in the swamp.  $8$ of them were eaten by frogs.   


**Which choice can be used to find how many fireflies were left?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blue{8}$ of the fireflies were eaten by frogs, so we use subtraction to take away $\blue{8}$.

$\red{12} - \blue{8}$

assessment_items.question_content:
$7$ witches live in the Boo Hoo Forest. $1$ of the witches is wicked and the rest are good.


**Which choice can be used to find how many witches are good?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blue1$ of them is wicked so we use subtraction to take away $\blue1$.



$\red{7} - \blue1$

assessment_items.question_content:
$10$ animals escaped from the zoo.  $3$ of them were monkeys and the rest were zebras.

**Which choice can be used to find how many zebras escaped from the zoo?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blue3$ of them were monkeys (not zebras) so we use subtraction to take away $\blue3$.


$\red{10} - \blue3$

assessment_items.question_content:
$12$ hippos were standing by the water. $2$ of them belly flopped into the water. 


**Which choice can be used to find how many hippos are still standing by the water?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blue{2}$ of them did a belly flop so we use subtraction to take away $\blue{2}$.


$\red{12} - \blue{2}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Tanya won $6$ games of checkers, and Mike won $4$ games. 

**Write a number sentence to show how many games of checkers Tanya and Mike played. **

 [[☃ input-number 3]] $ = 6 + $ [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7dc8bffb70c64f547f57ac479d3f9ab6.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green6 \text{ games Tanya won}\\
+& \underline{\blue4 \text{ games Mike won }}\\
=&\pink{10} \text{ games in total}\end{align}$
$\pink{10} = \green6 + \blue4 $

assessment_items.question_content:
$9$ dogs are in the park. $6$ more dogs join them.

**Write a number sentence to show how many dogs are now in the park.**

$9$ $ + $ [[☃ input-number 3]] $ = $ [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4d34ea0ec6a5e840223baf1a4a34d494.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green9 \text{ dogs}\\
+& \underline{\blue6 \text{ more dogs }}\\
=&\pink{15} \text{ dogs total}\end{align}$
$\green9 + \blue6 = \pink{15}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Jada added $9$ marshmallows to some hot chocolate, and Hunter added $7$ marshmallows.

**Write a number sentence to show how many marshmallows Jada and Hunter added.**

 [[☃ input-number 3]] $ = 9 + $ [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/229254542acf7fbed9bcd40606fe3131.png)

$\begin{align}&\green9 \text{ Jada's marshmallows}\\
+&  \underline{\blue7 \text{ Hunter's marshmallows }}\\
=&\pink{16} \text{ marshmallows in total}\end{align}$
$\pink{16} = \green9 + \blue7 $

assessment_items.question_content:
Ivy and Jo got dressed to go outside and play in the snow. They each put on $5$ layers of clothing.

**Write a number sentence to show how many layers of clothing Ivy and Jo were wearing all together. **

$5$ $ + $ [[☃ input-number 3]] $ = $ [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/82023b4a59d3a6521a6f84ad1a3e045d.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green5 \text{ Ivy's layers}\\
+&  \underline{\blue5 \text{ Jo's layers }}\\
=&\pink{10} \text{ total layers}\end{align}$
$\green5 + \blue5 = \pink{10}$

assessment_items.question_content:
On Monday James and his father started a new book and  read $12$ pages. On Tuesday they read $7$ pages to finish the book. 

**Write a number sentence to show how many pages were in the book. **

$12$ $ + $ [[☃ input-number 3]] $ = $ [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a85e350fb58b2c3a5aaa34400ea2e33.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{12} \text{ pages on Monday}\\
+&  \underline{ \,\,\blue7 \text{ pages on Tuesday }}\\
=&\pink{19} \text{ pages total}\end{align}$
$\green{12} + \blue7 = \pink{19}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Ed scored $8$ goals for his soccer team this season. Javi scored $5$ goals. 



**Write a number sentence to show how many goals Ed and Javi scored for their team this season. **

$8$ $ + $ [[☃ input-number 3]] $ = $ [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8d66723ef0eeb3622d88c062d3ef0493.png)

$\qquad \begin{align}&\green8 \text{ Ed's goals}\\
+&  \underline{\blue5 \text{ Javi's goals }}\\
=&\pink{13} \text{ total goals}\end{align}$
$\green8 + \blue5 = \pink{13}$

assessment_items.question_content:
I saw $9$ sea monsters with stripes and $5$ others with spots. 

**How many sea monsters did I see? ** 

[[☃ input-number 1]] sea monsters
hints:
$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{9} \text{ with stripes}\\
+&  \underline{\blue5 \text{ with spots}}\\
=&\pink{14} \text{ sea monsters in all}\end{align}$
I saw $\pink{14}$ sea monsters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ernie saw some snakes.

$6$ of them were dangerous, and $4$ were not. 

**How many snakes did Ernie see in all? ** 

[[☃ input-number 1]] snakes
hints:
$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{6} \text{ dangerous snakes}\\
+&  \underline{\blue4 \text{ not dangerous}}\\
=&\pink{10} \text{ snakes in total}\end{align}$
Ernie saw $\pink{10}$ snakes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Linda and Ava shared some popcorn for a snack. Linda took $6$ pieces of popcorn and Ava took $5$ pieces. 

**How many pieces of popcorn did they take in all? ** 

[[☃ input-number 1]] pieces of popcorn
hints:
$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{6} \text{ Linda's pieces}\\
+&  \underline{\blue5 \text{ Ava's pieces}}\\
=&\pink{11} \text{ pieces of popcorn all together}\end{align}$

Linda and 
Ava took $\pink{11}$ pieces of popcorn.

assessment_items.question_content:
My whole first grade class is eating ice cream today! $11$ students are having chocolate ice cream and $6$ other students are having vanilla.

**How many students are in my class? ** 

[[☃ input-number 1]] students
hints:
$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{11} \text{ will have chocolate ice cream}\\
+&  \underline{\,\,\,\blue6 \text{ will have vanilla ice cream}}\\
=&\pink{17} \text{ students in the class}\end{align}$
$\pink{17}$ students are in my class.

assessment_items.question_content:
Angela saw some ducks sitting by the pond. She saw $2$ white ducks and $9$ brown ducks.

**How many ducks did Angela see in all? ** 

[[☃ input-number 1]] ducks
hints:
$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{2} \text{ white ducks}\\
+&  \underline{\blue9 \text{ brown ducks}}\\
=&\pink{11} \text{ ducks in total}\end{align}$
Angela saw $\pink{11}$ ducks.

assessment_items.question_content:
The monkeys in the Fancy Forest always wear ties. $8$ of them wear bow ties and $4$ of them wear long ties.

**How many monkeys live in the Fancy Forest? ** 

[[☃ input-number 1]] monkeys
hints:
$\qquad \begin{align}&\green{8} \text{ monkeys with bow ties}\\
+&  \underline{\blue4 \text{ monkeys with long ties}}\\
=&\pink{12} \text{ monkeys in total}\end{align}$
$\pink{12}$ monkeys live in the Fancy Forest.

assessment_items.question_content:
From his boat, Stan saw $6$ whales and $2$ sharks.

**Which picture shows how many animals Stan saw? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Count $\blue{6}$ whales and $\green{2}$ sharks.

Stan saw $\pink{8}$ animals:

 ![6 whales. 2 sharks.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a7ef7543d7b01092cc2700af3b5932c.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Dad took me to the store to buy some fruit. I picked $3$ bananas and $3$ apples.

**Which picture shows how many pieces of fruit I picked? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Count $\blue{3}$ bananas and $\green{3}$ apples.

I picked $\pink{6}$ pieces of fruit:

 ![3 bananas. 3 apples.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/457e3c36e44d27aa320ad83e9c4cf050.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
I looked out the window and saw $8$ squirrels in the tree and $5$ squirrels running through the grass.


**Which picture shows how many squirrels I saw? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Count $\blue{8}$ squirrels and $\green{5}$ more squirrels.

I saw $\pink{13}$ squirrels:

![The first row has 3 squirrels. The second row has 10 squirrels.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c072e174fef3786a8ebedc0dc28489aa.png)

choices.content: ![The first row has 3 squirrels. The second row has 10 squirrels.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c072e174fef3786a8ebedc0dc28489aa.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Sophia got $4$ flower balloons and $3$ pony balloons for her birthday party. 


**Which picture shows how many balloons Sophia got in total? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Count $\blue{4}$ flower balloons and $\green{3}$ pony balloons.

Sophia got $\pink{7}$ balloons:

 ![4 flower balloons. 3 pony balloons.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d597e9ec4821d823d3af7f439db40dd1.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Leon brought out $6$ apples and $6$ slices of cheese for his friends that were playing in the backyard. 


**Which picture shows how many total snacks Leon brought out? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Count $\blue{6}$ apples and $\green{6}$ slices of cheese.

Leon brought out a total of $\pink{12}$ snacks:

 ![6 apples. 6 slices of cheese.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e4cb26862f4421322eb1258ec134cc0d.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Nick went to the zoo and saw $7$ monkeys and $3$ elephants.

**Which picture shows how many animals Nick saw at the zoo? **

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Count $\blue{7}$ monkeys and $\green{3}$ elephants.

Nick saw $\pink{10}$ animals:

 ![7 monkeys. 3 elephants.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/75cbf95c7b0dbd3a2d566155be0a0032.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
$10$ tigers were having a tea party. $4$ of them had to go home to finish their homework.

**How many tigers were left at the tea party? **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] tigers
hints:
$\pink{10}$ can be separated into two parts:  $\green{4}$ and $\blue?$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d84f30fa5a2d0998f17ae5ddc1550aee.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: 

$\pink{10} - \green{4} = \,\blue{?}$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\green{4}$ to get $\pink{10}$: 

$\green{4} + \,\blue{?} =
 \pink{10}$
$\blue{6}$ tigers were left at the party.

assessment_items.question_content:
$15$ ants were marching to a picnic at the park. Then $4$ of them saw a cookie in the grass and ran off to eat it.

**How many ants kept marching to the picnic? **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] ants
hints:
$\pink{15}$ can be separated into two parts:  $\green{4}$ and $\blue?$


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f2e9e429e66ca21d5da620021802e3e.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: 

$\pink{15} - \green{4} =\, \blue{?}$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\green{4}$ to get $\pink{15}$: 

$\green{4} + \,\blue{?} =
 \pink{15}$
$\blue{11}$ ants kept marching.

assessment_items.question_content:
$20$ monkeys were swinging in the trees. $5$ of them left to find some bananas. 

**How many monkeys were left swinging in the trees? **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] monkeys
hints:
$\pink{20}$ can be separated into two parts:  $\green{5}$ and $\blue?$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fefa40261b24bdf42f0f53bb9af059c8.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: 

$\pink{20} - \green{5} = \,\blue{?}$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\green{5}$ to get $\pink{20}$: 

$\green{5} + \,\blue{?} =
 \pink{20}$
$\blue{15}$ monkeys were left swinging.

assessment_items.question_content:
$8$ aliens landed on Mars. $6$ of them decided to leave because it was too cold.

**How many aliens were left on Mars? **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] aliens
hints:
$\pink{8}$ can be separated into two parts:  $\green{6}$ and $\blue?$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1b123ed9fa765486a6346ee1a4cc2ed7.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: 

$\pink{8} - \green{6} = \,\blue{?}$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\green{6}$ to get $\pink{8}$: 

$\green{6} + \,\blue{?} =
 \pink{8}$
$\blue{2}$ aliens were left on Mars.

assessment_items.question_content:
Green Grocery had $20$ cereal boxes this morning. Throughout the day people bought $18$ boxes. 

**How many cereal boxes are left at Green Grocery? **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] boxes
hints:
$\pink{20}$ can be separated into two parts:  $\green{18}$ and $\blue?$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/58c4462482568c8c67c68283282388c8.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: 

$\pink{20} - \green{18} = \,\blue?$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\green{18}$ to get $\pink{20}$:

 $\green{18} + \,\blue? = \pink{20}$
$\blue2$ boxes are left.

assessment_items.question_content:
$14$ superheroes met on the moon for a secret meeting. $3$ of them turned out to be evil villains in disguise. 

**How many were real superheroes? **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] superheroes
hints:
$\pink{14}$ can be separated into two parts:  $\green{3}$ and $\blue?$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e01dcdfb8a8a6b5a240e081566c6e20b.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: 

$\pink{14} - \green{3} = \,\blue{?}$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\green{3}$ to get $\pink{14}$: 

$\green{3} + \,\blue{?} =
 \pink{14}$
$\blue{11}$ were real superheroes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Peter the peacock had $17$ beautiful feathers in his tail. $9$ fell out.

**How many feathers are left in Peter's tail? **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] feathers
hints:
$\pink{17}$ can be separated into two parts:  $\green{9}$ and $\blue?$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/62a42b8df19c64414e8662cae093aff1.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: 

$\pink{17} - \green{9} = \,\blue{?}$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\green{9}$ to get $\pink{17}$: 

$\green{9} + \,\blue{?} =
 \pink{17}$
$\blue{8}$ feathers are left.

assessment_items.question_content:
$19$ birds were planning to sing in the concert.  $3$ of them had a sore throat and could not go.

**How many birds sang in the concert? **

 [[☃ input-number 1]] birds
hints:
$\pink{19}$ can be separated into two parts:  $\green{3}$ and $\blue?$

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5d8645940d4dadf5253b83b998e30280.png)

We can use subtraction to find the missing part: 

$\pink{19} - \green{3} = \,\blue{?}$

We can also think about how much more can be added to $\green{3}$ to get $\pink{19}$: 

$\green{3} + \,\blue{?} =
 \pink{19}$
$\blue{16}$ birds sang.
